title: Race-wide incident and cheap-pit proxies
summary: Safety Car/VSC or wet-transition laps make pit stops much cheaper and can cause clustered stops.
rationale: Safety Car/VSC or wet-transition laps make pit stops much cheaper and can cause clustered stops. The dataset lacks explicit flags/weather, but global lap-time spikes, compressed field pace, and many simultaneous position/stint changes can proxy these events.
implementation_hint: For each Race-LapNumber compute race_lap_median_time, median_time_vs_race_rolling_baseline, lap_time_iqr, count_large_position_changes, fraction_new_stint_or_pitstop_current_lap, and lagged versions shifted by one lap where feasible. Feed these as race-context features rather than only individual pace deviation.
expected_effect: May capture clustered pit waves that current individual tyre/degradation features miss.
risk: Using same-lap PitStop aggregate may be leakage if PitStop reflects stops on the current lap unavailable before predicting next lap; prefer lagged aggregates or validate both variants carefully.
